changes.old_value: Design Edition 1 limited to ~770 worldwide (~240 US) — MY2008 appearance package on Cayman S base
changes.new_value: Design Edition 1 limited to 777 worldwide (240 US) — MY2008 appearance package on Cayman S base
sources: stuttcars.com Cayman S Design Edition 1 2008 article
motorauthority.com Porsche Design Edition 1 Cayman S
changes.rationale: Production was exactly 777, not approximately 770. Confirmed by sequentially-numbered plaques in each car. Porsche intentionally chose 777 to reference the 35th anniversary of Porsche Design.

changes.old_value: Appearance edition based on the Cayman S. ~770 worldwide, approximately 240 US.
changes.new_value: Appearance edition based on the Cayman S. 777 worldwide, 240 US.
changes.rationale: Same correction as HIGH-01a, applied to variant description prose.

changes.old_value: 3.4L M97/21 flat-six (Cayman S) / 2.7L M96 flat-six (base Cayman)
changes.new_value: 3.4L M97/21 flat-six (Cayman S) / 2.7L M97/20 flat-six (base Cayman)
sources: Wikipedia 987 Cayman article: 'Base Cayman: 2.7L M97.20 flat-6'
changes.rationale: The 987.1 Cayman base used the M97/20 engine (not M96). The M96 was used in the 987.1 Boxster base (M96.26). The Cayman's 2.7L was a new-family M97 unit from launch, sharing the M97 family with the Cayman S (M97/21). Both share the IMS concern, but the engine code is distinct from the Boxster's M96.

changes.old_value: 2.7L M96 flat-six, 199 lb-ft, six-speed manual or five-speed Tiptronic. Base model that rounded out the lineup a model year after the S launch. Same M96 IMS exposure as the Boxster.
changes.new_value: 2.7L M97/20 flat-six, 201 lb-ft, five-speed manual or five-speed Tiptronic (six-speed optional). Base model that rounded out the lineup a model year after the S launch. Same M97 IMS exposure as the Boxster.
sources: Wikipedia 987 Boxster/Cayman: 'M97.20 flat-6, 201 lb-ft; 5-speed manual (G87.01) standard, 6-speed manual optional'
changes.rationale: Three sub-corrections: (1) engine code M96 → M97/20; (2) torque 199 → 201 lb-ft (272 Nm, not 270 Nm as implied by 199); (3) standard gearbox was 5-speed (G87.01), not 6-speed — 6-speed (A87.20) was a paid option. The Cayman S used 6-speed standard; the base did not.

changes.old_value: Cayman S Black Edition (MY2012): ~500 US units, appearance package with R engine output — manual or PDK
changes.new_value: Cayman S Black Edition (MY2012): ~500 worldwide (~180 US), appearance package with R engine output — manual or PDK
sources: Excellence Magazine: 'Cayman S Black Edition: Limited to 500'
Rennlist registry thread: '2012 Cayman S Black Edition - #209 of 500'
stuttcars.com Cayman S Black Edition page: '500 units'
Multiple auction listings confirming worldwide production plaques
changes.rationale: The 500-unit production cap is worldwide, not US-only. Individual plaques read 'N of 500' — this is the total worldwide build. US allocation was approximately 180 units. The original content's '~500 US units' would imply nearly all production went to the US, which contradicts the numbered registry evidence.

changes.location: variants[3] — Cayman S Black Edition description
changes.old_value: Final-run appearance edition with Cayman R engine output (330 hp via ECU remap). ~500 US units.
changes.new_value: Final-run appearance edition with Cayman R engine output (330 hp via ECU remap). ~500 worldwide (~180 US).
changes.rationale: Same correction as HIGH-05a, applied to variant description.
